paper_title: Rethinking Sparse Mixture of Experts from a Unified Perspective

Token Choice.advantage: Ability to dynamically select experts for each token.

Expert Choice.advantage: Ensures a more balanced token distribution across experts.

evaluation_results.performance: USMoE outperforms both Token Choice and Expert Choice across six tasks.
evaluation_results.training_status: Outperforms in non-fine-tuned settings ('without additional training') and also in fine-tuned and pretraining settings.
evaluation_results.strong_performance_areas: Tasks requiring deep input understanding, such as semantic textual similarity, classification, and clustering.

answer_to_user_question.summary: Based on the provided text, neither Token Choice nor Expert Choice is presented as definitively better. The paper identifies key limitations in both: Token Choice suffers from unbalanced expert loading, while Expert Choice suffers from token dropping.
answer_to_user_question.paper_conclusion: The paper proposes a new method, USMoE, which it demonstrates outperforms both traditional approaches (Token Choice and Expert Choice) across multiple tasks.
answer_to_user_question.generalization_performance: USMoE consistently outperforms both Token Choice and Expert Choice in generalization across multiple tasks, both with (fine-tuned) and without (non-fine-tuned) additional training. Its gains are particularly strong in tasks requiring deep input understanding.

title: Rethinking Sparse Mixture of Experts from a Unified Perspective
